title: Tragacantha tigridis
taxonomy: Tragacantha tigridis is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic flower structure and its seed pods. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Tragacantha tigridis shares similarities with other plants in this family, such as pea trees and lupines. The genus Tragacantha is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises most of the legume species. Within the Faboideae subfamily, Tragacantha tigridis is further grouped based on its specific characteristics and traits.
uses: Tragacantha tigridis, also known as the goat's thorn or Kalihiri, has been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. In Ayurvedic medicine, Tragacantha tigridis is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: There is no information available about the plant "Tragacantha tigridis". However, Tragacanth, also known as Tragacanth gum or Tragacantha, is a type of natural adhesive derived from the sap of the Astragalus gummifer or other species of the genus Astragalus. This plant is used as a gelling agent in various food products and pharmaceuticals. Tragacanth gum has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it has been found to contain bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions, as well as oral supplements for digestive and respiratory issues.
